channel.title: Your RSS Feed Title
channel.description: A brief description of your RSS feed.

item.title: socials in the terminal, best of RSS with newsboat
item.description: Spending too much time 'surfing' and you are capable of using linux ? Well waste no more time. If you are a sucker for minimal software and text based user interfaces turn you on then newsboar is for you. A simple terminal application that updates and relays your subscibed RSS feeds in the terminal. Real blogs and articles straight from the command line. The setup is also childs play as you only need to append the rss feed url in the ~/url config file located in ~/,config/newsboat/url or ~/.newsboat/url depending on where newsboat is installed.

item.title: peasant hacker , the easy way to bypass FRP ( Factory Reset Protection )
item.description: Back again with a well known secret from the south , bypassing FRP, well after Google realised motherfuckers are doing some crip hand signs to their devices to boot them into recovery mode and access the " Factory format / reset option " and being able to completly erase the device's data together with the secuirty lock . The FRP was introduced. Simply this feature makes it harder to use a stolen device after factory reset; Just like iCloud but not quite. This is enabled by default on devices running android 5 and above.

item.title: Too much pressure or zoomer android dev mistakes ?
item.description:
After endless hours of binge watching and following the YouTube algorithm, decided to look at what was trending on my favourite social media site, github !. Ahh jadx got some updated. It has been a while since I last looked into an android application. This was a pretty short , sweet and sad story to the developers of the mobile loan lending app. 'Ganji" the application name literally meaning money translated from 'sheng - kenyan slang'. Unfortunately either the company didn't have money to employ competent devs or the devs are paid shit or

deadlines & pressure

we've given enough excuses for this company; This is the easy part.

item.title: Automating Dating App Swipes
item.description:
We've all tried dating apps even if it's with a psudo account. Most of them now emulate one common structure in which the app interacts with the user. 
After successfull sacrifice of your personal information , you will be required to swipe left / right, tap on a love icon if a profile you've come across peaks your interest :-) . And cross or vice versa if you don't like a profile. Due to desperation or 'time' almost everyone swipes right on any profile quickly to pass the whole reading a profile's bio and shit like that.

What if you got a badoo premium subscribtion for a week and want to maximise the 'unlimited' swipes
you get after paying $1
